title: Accessing and cleaning federal fisheries data
text: package contains various functions accessing (pulling) cleaning federal fisheries dependent independent data. Many data sets confidential require formal data request submission appropriate governing body. Information regarding metadata access data sets can accessed via inPort, see : InPort authoritative metadata repository data inventory platform NOAA Fisheries National Ocean Service. system supports documentation datasets provides tools facilitate data discovery, public access, responsible stewardship scientific data within line offices. , using species.shifts require access data order use cleaning plotting functions. strongly recommended data stored locally centralized repository. Please refer package README information recommended work flows.

title: Accessing and cleaning federal fisheries data
text: functions package correspond six federal fisheries data sets, respective pull_() function. NOAA Fisheries Vessel Trip Reports NEFSC Observer Sea NEFSC Spring-Fall Bottom Trawl Survey GARFO Permits Data GARFO Dealer Reported Landings NOAA Fisheries Marine Recreational Information Program vtr <- pull_vtr(proj_path = my_path) *Trip ID information removed demo maintain confidentiality. observer <- pull_observer(proj_path = my_path) *Trip ID information removed demo maintain confidentiality. nefsc <- pull_nefsc(proj_path = my_path) permits <- pull_permits(proj_path = my_path) landings <- pull_landings(proj_path = my_path) mrip_directed_trips <- pull_mrip_directed_trips() mrip_catch <- pull_mrip_catch()

title: Plotting federal fisheries data
text: Upon running respective pull_() functions, subsequent plot_() map_() functions allow users visualize data species featured species_list(). strongly advised save outputs pull_() functions locally, data pulling functions take quite run. Saving locally ensure plotting, minor aesthetic revisions done quickly efficiently. examples plots maps can generated federal data set. functions written within tidyverse can edited intermediate understanding R.

title: Plotting federal fisheries data
text: plotting function vessel trip reports yields four-paneled map, depicts leading trailing edges catch distribution. edges represented 10th 90th percentiles latitude, weighted kept catch. center represent 80% kept catch. yellow contour lines represent density kept catch, closer lines representing greater density activity. VTR data cropped management councils minimize effects outliers inaccurate reports.
code: vtr <- pull_vtr(proj_path = my_path) map_vtr(species = summer flounder", data = vtr) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder vtr.png"

title: Plotting federal fisheries data
text: Similiar vessel trip reports, maps represent leading trailing edges observations. 10th 90th percentile reported latitudes, weighted live weight (biomass) fish observed. center represents 80% observed biomass contour lines depict density observations.
code: obs <- pull_obs(proj_path = my_path) map_obs(species = summer flounder", data = obs) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder observer map.png"

title: Plotting federal fisheries data
text: multiple plotting functions visualize dealer reported federal landings. first shows overall trends lan ded volume (weight) value particular species across east coast United States. state landings plotting function returns proportion landings across states landed value volume. council landings plotting function returns proportion landings across management councils landed value volume.
code: landings <- pull_garfo_landings(proj_path = my_path) plot_landings_trends(species = "summer flounder", data = obs) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder landings.png" plot_state_landings(species = "summer flounder", data = landings) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder state landings.png" plot_council_landings(species = "summer flounder", data = landings) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder council landings.png"

title: Plotting federal fisheries data
text: Catch estimates, different directed trip estimates, provided estimate amount recreational species harvested. plotting function catch estimate returns summary table, describing presence, total harvest, harvest trends, proportions harvest particular species. Similar catch estimates, directed trips gives rough estimate recreational fishing effort particular species. directed trips plotting function also returns summary table, depicting proportions directed trips state, fishing area, trip type, trends time.

Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder catch.png" directed_trips <- get_mrip_directed_trips() plot_directed_trips(species = "summer flounder", data = directed_trips) Error in `knitr::include_graphics()`: ! Cannot find the file(s): "../images/summer flounder directed trips.png"

what: Northeast US Marine Species Distribution Shifts
title: Northeast US Marine Species Distribution Shifts
text: goal species.shifts consolidate variety federal data sets commonly used Gulf Maine Research Institute Portland, Maine. Many data sets support collaborations New England, Mid-Atlantic, South Atlantic Fisheries Management Councils. package pulls, cleans, analyzes data sets pre-existing data repository. Many data sets confidential /require explicit data request relevant bodies. Given data acquisition, recommended store data central repository ease access within R functions.

what: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
text: Downloads processes MRIP intercept survey data estimate directed fishing trips species, state, mode, area along US East Coast.

title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
code: pull_mrip_directed_trips(   species = c("ATLANTIC CROAKER", "ATLANTIC MACKEREL", "BLACK SEA BASS",     "SPANISH MACKEREL", "STRIPED BASS", "SUMMER FLOUNDER", "SCUP", "SPINY DOGFISH",     "GOOSEFISH", "TILEFISH", "BLUELINE TILEFISH", "BLUEFISH", "GRAY TRIGGERFISH",     "KING MACKEREL"),   states = c(23, 33, 25, 44, 9, 10, 24, 34, 36, 51, 37, 45, 13, 121),   start_year = 2010,   end_year = 2024,   trip_type = 1,   domain = list(wave = list(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6))),   data_dir = here::here("data", "MRIP_Index"),   download = TRUE )

title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
text: species Character vector species common names (uppercase). E.g., c("STRIPED BASS", "BLUEFISH"). states Integer vector FIPS-style MRIP state codes. Defaults East Coast states: , NH, MA, RI, CT, DE, MD, NJ, NY, VA, NC, SC, GA, FL (east coast). start_year Integer. First year data retrieve. Default 2010. end_year Integer. Last year data retrieve. Default 2024. trip_type Integer scalar (1–5). Trip definition passed MRIP.dirtrips(). 1 = primary target (default). domain List defining domain strata passed MRIP.dirtrips(). Defaults six waves: list(wave = list(c(1:6))). data_dir Path directory raw MRIP zip files extracted CSVs stored. Defaults ::("data", "MRIP_Index"). download Logical. TRUE (default), scrape NMFS download missing zip files processing. output_path Path processed CSV output file. Set NULL skip writing. Defaults ::("data", "processed", "mrip_primary_target.csv").

title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
text: tibble one row per species-domain   combination, containing trip estimates, standard errors, PSEs,   human-readable state, mode, area columns.   Invisibly also writes CSV output_path NULL.

title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
text: function performs three steps: Download: Scrapes NMFS MRIP CSV directory     ps_*.zip files downloads missing locally. Organise: Moves extracted CSVs int<YYYY>     sub-folders expected MRIP.dirtrips(). Estimate: Calls MRIP.dirtrips() per species,     row-binds results, attaches readable labels state, fishing     mode, area. Processing East Coast states many years can take several hours.

title: Extract MRIP Directed Trip Estimates for Multiple Species — pull_mrip_directed_trips
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Quick run: two species, Mid-Atlantic only mid_atl <- c(10, 24, 34, 36, 51)  trips <- get_mrip_directed_trips(   species    = c("STRIPED BASS", "SUMMER FLOUNDER"),   states     = mid_atl,   start_year = 2018,   end_year   = 2022 )  # Full East Coast run (warning: ~4 hours) trips_all <- get_mrip_directed_trips(   species = c("BLUEFISH", "BLACK SEA BASS", "SCUP") ) } # }

text: Raw cleaned common names species managed MAFMC across six federal data sets.

title: Mid-Atlantic Species — species_list
Mid-Atlantic Species — species_list
text: source Filter desired data set. source = "" returns complete list. Options include "nefsc, vtr, observer, landings, mrip, permits"

title: Mid-Atlantic Species — species_list
text: Data frame landings species, principal port state, landings, live weight, value, latitude longitude principal port.

title: Mid-Atlantic Species — species_list
code: species_list(source = "all") #> Error in dplyr::mutate(data.frame(comname = c("mackerel, atlantic", "mackerel, chub",     "croaker, atlantic", "striped bass", "black sea bass", "tilefish",     "bluefish", "butterfish", "monkfish / anglerfish / goosefish",     "scup / porgy", "dogfish, spiny", "flounder, summer / fluke",     "tilefish, blueline", "mackerel, spanish", "mackerel, king",     "ocean quahog", "clam, surf", "squid / loligo", "squid / illex")),     clean_name = stringr::str_remove(comname, pattern = " /.*"),     clean_name = SwimmeR::name_reorder(clean_name), clean_name = dplyr::case_when(comname ==         "squid / illex" ~ "northern shortfin squid", comname ==         "squid / loligo" ~ "longfin squid", .default = clean_name),     data_source = "vtr"): ℹ In argument: `clean_name = SwimmeR::name_reorder(clean_name)`. #> Caused by error in `loadNamespace()`: #> ! there is no package called ‘SwimmeR’
